name: Grade 4 Science: Inquiry and Evidence — Semester 2
description: A rich Grade 4 science pathway for engineering design, data analysis, models, and explanatory writing. Learners investigate phenomena through observation, data, models, engineering design, and evidence-based explanation.
learningGoals: Investigate engineering design, data analysis, models, and explanatory writing through questions, observations, measurements, and data.
Use models, patterns, and scientific reasoning to explain or predict phenomena.
Apply safe, ethical scientific and engineering practices.
Communicate a claim with evidence, reasoning, limitations, and a next question.

lessons.title: Phenomenon launch
lessons.objective: I can observe a phenomenon, record relevant details, and frame a question while studying engineering design, data analysis, models, and explanatory writing.

lessons.title: Evidence lab
lessons.objective: I can collect and organize evidence through a safe investigation or data activity while studying engineering design, data analysis, models, and explanatory writing.

lessons.title: Model and explanation studio
lessons.objective: I can build, test, or revise a model, explanation, or design solution while studying engineering design, data analysis, models, and explanatory writing.

resources.title: Next Generation Science Standards: Three-Dimensional Learning
resources.description: Framework reference for integrating practices, core ideas, and crosscutting concepts.

resources.title: NGSS Appendices: Practices, Crosscutting Concepts, and Engineering
USGS Data in Schools
resources.description: Data-literacy resources and scientific visualizations for students.

resources.title: NASA STEM Learning Resources
resources.description: Optional grade-banded science and engineering investigations.

units.title: Observe a phenomenon: engineering design, data analysis, models, and explanatory writing
units.essentialQuestion: What is happening, and what evidence can we gather?
outcomes: Make careful observations, identify patterns, and ask testable questions.
Use evidence responsibly and revise explanations or designs when new evidence matters.
standardIds: NGSS: Grades 3-5 science and engineering practices
NGSS: Crosscutting concepts and engineering design

lessons.title: Phenomenon launch
lessons.objective: I can observe a phenomenon, record relevant details, and frame a question while studying engineering design, data analysis, models, and explanatory writing.

activities.title: Notice and question
activities.learnerAction: the learner will observe a phenomenon, record relevant details, and frame a question. Follow laboratory, outdoor, tool, material, and digital-data safety directions; record observations honestly, including results that differ from predictions.

successCriteria: I use an observation, measurement, data point, or model detail as evidence.
I explain a pattern, cause, system, or design choice with age-appropriate scientific vocabulary.
I identify a limitation, safety consideration, or next question for the investigation.

lessons.title: Evidence lab
lessons.objective: I can collect and organize evidence through a safe investigation or data activity while studying engineering design, data analysis, models, and explanatory writing.

successCriteria: I use an observation, measurement, data point, or model detail as evidence.
I explain a pattern, cause, system, or design choice with age-appropriate scientific vocabulary.
I identify a limitation, safety consideration, or next question for the investigation.

lessons.title: Model and explanation studio
lessons.objective: I can build, test, or revise a model, explanation, or design solution while studying engineering design, data analysis, models, and explanatory writing.

successCriteria: I use an observation, measurement, data point, or model detail as evidence.

units.title: Investigate fairly: engineering design, data analysis, models, and explanatory writing
units.essentialQuestion: How can we collect trustworthy evidence?
outcomes: Plan a safe investigation, measure or record observations, and consider variables or repeated trials when developmentally appropriate.
Use evidence responsibly and revise explanations or designs when new evidence matters.
standardIds: NGSS: Grades 3-5 science and engineering practices
NGSS: Crosscutting concepts and engineering design

units.title: Model and design: engineering design, data analysis, models, and explanatory writing
units.essentialQuestion: How can models or designed solutions help us explain or improve a system?
outcomes: Use a diagram, physical model, data display, or prototype to explain, predict, or test a solution.
Use evidence responsibly and revise explanations or designs when new evidence matters.
standardIds: NGSS: Grades 3-5 science and engineering practices
NGSS: Crosscutting concepts and engineering design

units.title: Explain and communicate: engineering design, data analysis, models, and explanatory writing
units.essentialQuestion: What claim can our evidence support, and what are its limits?
outcomes: Construct an evidence-based explanation, revise after feedback, and communicate uncertainty, next steps, or tradeoffs.
Use evidence responsibly and revise explanations or designs when new evidence matters.
standardIds: NGSS: Grades 3-5 science and engineering practices
NGSS: Crosscutting concepts and engineering design
